response:
a father has two children and wishes to improve their engagement with literature over pop culture. he decides to introduce a structured reading schedule to maximize their exposure to literature. over a period of 30 days, he sets a reading challenge where each day, the children will read a number of pages that follows a specific mathematical relationship.  

the number of pages read each day follows a quadratic sequence. if on the first day, the children read 5 pages, on the second day 12 pages, and on the third day 21 pages, the general formula for the number of pages p(n) read on the n-th day can be derived by analyzing the sequence. the sequence shows that the difference between consecutive terms increases by 7 each time. this indicates a quadratic relationship, where the second difference is constant. the formula for the sequence is p(n) = n² + 3n - 1.  

using the formula derived in part (1), the total number of pages the children will read by the end of the 30 days can be calculated by summing the sequence from n = 1 to n = 30. the sum of the sequence is given by the formula for the sum of the first n terms of a quadratic sequence, which is (n(n + 1)(2n + 1))/6 + (3n(n + 1))/2 - n. substituting n = 30 into this formula gives the total number of pages read over the 30 days.
